description: Phase 3 Core Trove Operations — Open-trove form (collateral, debt, interest rate, chart, loan summary), manage-trove view (adjust, rate change, close), all wired to existing Phase 2 write hooks

userStories.title: Create form state atoms for open-trove
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Jotai atoms to hold the open-trove form state so inputs are preserved during the flow dialog and across component renders.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/atoms/trove-form-atoms.ts
Define openTroveFormAtom with shape: { collAmount: string, debtAmount: string, interestRate: string } — strings for input fields, converted to bigint on submit
Default values: { collAmount: '', debtAmount: '', interestRate: '' }
Use plain atom() from Jotai (no persistence needed)
Export from atoms/index.ts barrel
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
userStories.notes: Atoms barrel is at packages/web3/src/features/borrow/atoms/index.ts — add alphabetically. Existing atoms: deployment-atoms.ts, flow-atoms.ts.

userStories.title: Create collateral input component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enter the amount of USDm collateral to deposit, see my wallet balance, and use a Max button.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/collateral-input.tsx
Shows 'USDm' token label and 'Collateral' heading
Numeric input field for collateral amount (string state)
Displays user's USDm wallet balance (use wagmi useBalance or equivalent existing pattern)
Max button sets input to wallet balance (or balance minus a small gas buffer)
Input accepts only valid numeric values (positive numbers, decimals)
Controlled component: accepts value: string and onChange: (value: string) => void props
Uses Tailwind + @repo/ui Card for consistent styling with existing borrow components
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Check how pool forms handle token balance display — reference apps/app.mento.org/app/components/pools/add-liquidity-form.tsx for patterns. USDm is the collateral token across all borrow deployments.

userStories.title: Create debt input component with suggestions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enter the amount of GBPm to borrow and see suggested amounts based on my collateral.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/debt-input.tsx
Shows debt token symbol from selectedDebtTokenAtom (e.g., 'GBPm') and 'Borrow' heading
Numeric input field for debt amount
Shows minimum debt from useSystemParams() as helper text below input (e.g., 'Min: £2,000')
Suggestion chips from useDebtSuggestions(collAmount, price) — clickable buttons that fill the input
Amounts formatted using debt token locale from DEBT_TOKEN_CONFIGS (e.g., '£1,234.56')
Controlled component: accepts value: string, onChange: (value: string) => void, and collAmount: bigint props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useDebtSuggestions and useSystemParams are already built in Phase 1. formatDebtAmount from features/borrow/format.ts handles locale formatting. parseUnits/formatUnits from viem for bigint conversion.

userStories.title: Create interest rate input component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set my annual interest rate with a slider and manual entry, seeing the minimum rate and annual cost estimate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/interest-rate-input.tsx
Shows 'Annual Interest Rate' heading
Slider component from @repo/ui with range: min from useSystemParams().minInterestRate to a reasonable max (e.g., 15%)
Manual numeric input field synced with slider — editing one updates the other
Display current rate as percentage (e.g., '5.5%')
Show annual cost estimate: debtAmount * rate, formatted in debt token currency using formatDebtAmount
Show redemption risk from useRedemptionRisk(rate) as a text label or RiskBadge
Controlled component: accepts value: string, onChange: (value: string) => void, and debtAmount: bigint props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: @repo/ui Slider is Radix-based: props are min, max, step, value (array), onValueChange. Interest rates are stored as 18-decimal bigints in the SDK (e.g., 5% = 5n * 10n**16n). useRedemptionRisk and RiskBadge already exist from Phase 1.

userStories.title: Create interest rate chart component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a mini bar chart showing how debt is distributed across interest rate brackets so I can understand where my rate falls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/interest-rate-chart.tsx
Uses Recharts via @repo/ui Chart components (ChartContainer etc.)
Data from useInterestRateChartData(selectedRate) hook — returns { rate: number, debt: number, isCurrentRate: boolean }[]
Bar chart with x-axis = interest rate, y-axis = total debt at that rate
Highlights the bar matching the user's selected rate with a different color
Compact size suitable for embedding in the form (roughly 200-300px height)
Accepts selectedRate: string prop
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Recharts 3.6.0 is in packages/ui. Chart wrapper at packages/ui/src/components/ui/chart.tsx exports ChartContainer with theme-aware colors. useInterestRateChartData already exists from Phase 1.

userStories.title: Create loan summary component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a real-time summary of my loan metrics (LTV, liquidation price, risk level, fees) as I fill out the form.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/loan-summary.tsx
Uses useLoanDetails(collAmount, debtAmount, price) for real-time metrics
Displays: LTV (formatted %), liquidation price (formatted in debt token currency), risk level via RiskBadge, collateral ratio
Shows one-time fee from usePredictUpfrontFee(debtAmount, interestRate) formatted in debt token currency
Shows annual interest cost: debtAmount * rate formatted in debt token currency
Updates in real-time as form inputs change
Grayed out / placeholder state when inputs are empty or insufficient
Accepts collAmount: bigint, debtAmount: bigint, interestRate: bigint props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useLoanDetails, usePredictUpfrontFee, and RiskBadge already exist. TroveMetrics component in shared/ can be reused or referenced for layout. formatDebtAmount, formatCollateralAmount, formatLtv, formatInterestRate from features/borrow/format.ts.

userStories.title: Create open-trove form container and wire to hook
userStories.description: As a user, I want a complete form to open a new trove with all inputs, summary, and submit button wired to the useOpenTrove hook.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/open-trove/open-trove-form.tsx
Composes: CollateralInput, DebtInput, InterestRateInput, InterestRateChart, LoanSummary
Form state: local state or openTroveFormAtom — collAmount, debtAmount, interestRate as strings
'Open Trove' submit button at bottom
Button disabled with descriptive text when: wallet not connected, inputs empty, below min debt, insufficient balance

On submit: converts strings to bigints via parseUnits, gets ownerIndex from useNextOwnerIndex(), computes maxUpfrontFee (fee estimate + 5% buffer), calls openTrove.mutate({ symbol, params: { owner, ownerIndex, collAmount, boldAmount, annualInterestRate, maxUpfrontFee }, wagmiConfig, account })
After mutation triggers: flow dialog shows automatically (via borrowFlowAtom)
'Back to Dashboard' button/link that sets borrowViewAtom to 'dashboard'
Card-based layout with sections for each input group
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useOpenTrove accepts { symbol, params: OpenTroveParams, wagmiConfig, account }. OpenTroveParams: { owner, ownerIndex, collAmount, boldAmount, annualInterestRate, maxUpfrontFee }. wagmiConfig from useConfig(), account from useAccount(). selectedDebtTokenAtom provides symbol. useNextOwnerIndex(symbol, owner) provides ownerIndex.

userStories.title: Wire open-trove form into borrow-view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate to the open-trove form from the dashboard and see the full form.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/borrow-view.tsx
Replace the open-trove placeholder (the 'Coming soon' section for open-trove view) with <OpenTroveForm />
Import OpenTroveForm from ./open-trove/open-trove-form
Clicking 'Open Trove' button on dashboard navigates to the form (borrowViewAtom = 'open-trove')
Back button on form returns to dashboard
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: borrow-view.tsx already has a switch on borrowViewAtom — just replace the placeholder case for 'open-trove'. Dashboard 'Open Trove' button already sets borrowViewAtom to 'open-trove'.

userStories.title: Create manage-trove view container with tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want a tabbed view to manage my existing trove with options to adjust, change rate, or close.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/manage-trove/manage-trove-view.tsx
Accepts troveId: string prop (from borrowViewAtom state: { view: 'manage-trove', troveId })
Uses useTroveData(symbol, troveId) to load current trove data
Header showing current trove summary: collateral, debt, interest rate, risk level using existing format utils and RiskBadge
Tab navigation using @repo/ui Tabs: 'Adjust' | 'Interest Rate' | 'Close'
Renders placeholder content for each tab (actual forms wired in subsequent stories)
Loading state while trove data loads (skeleton or spinner)
'Back to Dashboard' button that sets borrowViewAtom to 'dashboard'
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: @repo/ui Tabs pattern: Tabs > TabsList > TabsTrigger + TabsContent. useTroveData(symbol, troveId) returns BorrowPosition. selectedDebtTokenAtom provides symbol. TroveMetrics from shared/ can display the header metrics.

userStories.title: Create adjust trove form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add/remove collateral and borrow/repay debt on my existing trove, seeing before/after comparisons.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/manage-trove/adjust-form.tsx
Two sections: Collateral adjustment and Debt adjustment
Each section has direction toggle: 'Add' / 'Remove' for collateral, 'Borrow more' / 'Repay' for debt
Collateral input: amount field, shows wallet balance (add) or current collateral (remove)
Debt input: amount field, shows current debt, min debt constraint
Before → After comparison: current LTV → new LTV, current liquidation price → new using useLoanDetails for both states
Fee estimate via usePredictUpfrontFee if borrowing more (debt increase triggers fee)
'Adjust Trove' submit button with disabled states (nothing changed, below min debt, exceeds balance)
On submit: builds AdjustTroveParams { troveId, collChange, isCollIncrease, debtChange, isDebtIncrease, maxUpfrontFee }, calls adjustTrove.mutate({ symbol, params, wagmiConfig, account })
Accepts troveId: string and troveData: BorrowPosition props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useAdjustTrove accepts { symbol, params: AdjustTroveParams, wagmiConfig, account }. AdjustTroveParams: { troveId, collChange, isCollIncrease, debtChange, isDebtIncrease, maxUpfrontFee } — no owner field, account passed separately. collChange and debtChange are absolute amounts (not deltas from current).

userStories.title: Create interest rate change form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change my trove's interest rate, seeing the fee impact and new annual cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/manage-trove/rate-form.tsx
Shows current interest rate prominently
New rate input: slider + manual entry (similar pattern to InterestRateInput from open-trove)
Before → After comparison: current annual cost → new annual cost
Redemption risk indicator for the new rate using useRedemptionRisk
Fee estimate for rate change via usePredictUpfrontFee (rate decreases may trigger upfront fee)
'Change Rate' submit button with disabled states (rate unchanged, below minimum)
On submit: calls adjustInterestRate.mutate({ symbol, troveId, newRate, maxUpfrontFee, wagmiConfig, account })
Accepts troveId: string and troveData: BorrowPosition props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useAdjustInterestRate accepts { symbol, troveId, newRate, maxUpfrontFee, wagmiConfig, account }. newRate is bigint (18-decimal). Interest rate stored as annualInterestRate on BorrowPosition.

userStories.title: Create close trove form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to close my trove, seeing exactly how much debt I need to repay and collateral I'll receive.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/manage-trove/close-form.tsx
Shows summary: total debt to repay (from troveData.debt + accrued interest), collateral to receive back (troveData.collateral)
Wallet balance check: shows warning if user's debt token balance is insufficient to repay
Clear confirmation messaging: 'You will repay £X and receive Y USDm'
'Close Trove' submit button with destructive variant (red/warning styling via Button variant='destructive')
Button disabled if wallet balance insufficient
On submit: calls closeTrove.mutate({ symbol, troveId, wagmiConfig, account })
On success: flow dialog handles navigation back to dashboard automatically
Accepts troveId: string and troveData: BorrowPosition props
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useCloseTrove accepts { symbol, troveId, wagmiConfig, account }. The hook handles debt token approval internally (approves BorrowerOperations with maxUint256). FlowDialog 'Back to Dashboard' button navigates automatically.

userStories.title: Wire manage-trove view into borrow-view and connect tab forms
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a position card on the dashboard and see the manage-trove view with all three tab forms working.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/borrow-view.tsx — replace manage-trove placeholder with <ManageTroveView troveId={view.troveId} />
Import ManageTroveView from ./manage-trove/manage-trove-view
Update manage-trove-view.tsx to render actual forms in tabs: AdjustForm in 'Adjust' tab, RateForm in 'Interest Rate' tab, CloseForm in 'Close' tab — pass troveId and troveData to each
Verify: clicking position card on dashboard navigates to manage view with correct troveId
Verify: Back to Dashboard returns to dashboard
Verify: all three tabs render their forms correctly
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: borrow-view.tsx discriminates manage-trove via typeof view === 'object'. Position card already sets borrowViewAtom to { view: 'manage-trove', troveId }. ManageTroveView was created in US-009 with placeholder tabs — now wire the real forms.

userStories.title: Add claim collateral button to dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to claim surplus collateral directly from the dashboard when available.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/dashboard/borrow-dashboard.tsx
Check if user has claimable collateral — look at useUserTroves data for troves with closedByLiquidation status or surplus collateral indicator
If claimable: show a 'Claim Collateral' button or info banner on the dashboard
On click: calls claimCollateral.mutate({ symbol, wagmiConfig, account }) — useClaimCollateral hook
Flow dialog shows claim progress automatically
If no claimable collateral: button/banner not shown
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: useClaimCollateral accepts { symbol, wagmiConfig, account }. Single-step flow — sdk.buildClaimCollateralTransaction(symbol). This may be hard to test visually since it requires a liquidated trove with surplus — just ensure the UI renders conditionally and the hook is wired correctly.
